ucs.title: [LLM] Fox Kitten / Pioneer Kitten reverse-tunnel tool execution (ngrok, FRP, plink, chisel) post edge-appliance compromise
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Fox Kitten / Pioneer Kitten / Lemon Sandstorm post-exploitation step: after exploiting an internet-facing VPN / appliance (Pulse Secure CVE-2019-11510, Citrix CVE-2019-19781, Fortinet CVE-2018-13379, F5 BIG-IP, Palo Alto GlobalProtect), the operator drops a reverse-tunneling client (ngrok, Fast Reverse Proxy 'frpc', plink, chisel, ssf, revsocks) and exposes internal services back to attacker infrastructure. CISA AA20-259A and CrowdStrike specifically call out FRP and ngrok as Pioneer Kitten staples for selling/handing off access.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND (Processes.process_name IN ("ngrok.exe","frpc.exe","frps.exe","plink.exe","chisel.exe","ssf.exe","revsocks.exe","gost.exe") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") AND (Processes.process="*ngrok*" OR Processes.process="*frpc*" OR Processes.process="*plink*" OR Processes.process="*chisel*"))) AND (Processes.process="*authtoken*" OR Processes.process="* -R *" OR Processes.process="*--remote*" OR Processes.process="*tunnel*" OR Processes.process="*-c frpc*" OR Processes.process="*--config*frpc*" OR Processes.process="*tcp 3389*" OR Processes.process="*tcp 445*" OR Processes.process="* -L *") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | join type=outer dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (443,7000,7001,4443,8080,1080) AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest_category="internal") by All_Traffic.src | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as dest ] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let TunnelBins = dynamic(["ngrok.exe","frpc.exe","frps.exe","plink.exe","chisel.exe","ssf.exe","revsocks.exe","gost.exe"]);
let TunnelArgs = dynamic(["authtoken"," -R ","--remote","tunnel","-c frpc","--config","tcp 3389","tcp 445","tcp 22"," -L ","--reverse","--server"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName in~ (TunnelBins))
    or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
        and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ngrok","frpc","plink","chisel","revsocks"))
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (TunnelArgs)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemotePort in (443, 7000, 7001, 4443, 8080, 1080, 22)
    | project DeviceId, NetTime = Timestamp, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl, NetProc = InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(NetTime) or NetTime between (Timestamp - 5m .. Timestamp + 30m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let TunnelBins = dynamic(["ngrok.exe","frpc.exe","frps.exe","plink.exe","chisel.exe","ssf.exe","revsocks.exe","gost.exe"]);
let TunnelArgs = dynamic(["authtoken"," -R ","--remote","tunnel","-c frpc","--config","tcp 3389","tcp 445","tcp 22"," -L ","--reverse","--server"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ProcName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ProcName in (TunnelBins)
    or (ProcName in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
        and CommandLine has_any ("ngrok","frpc","plink","chisel","revsocks"))
| where CommandLine has_any (TunnelArgs)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Fox Kitten Reverse-Tunnel Tool Execution (ngrok / FRP / plink / chisel)
id: 8b3a1d22-8fde-4d14-9a2a-foxk-tunnel-01
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of reverse-tunneling utilities used by Fox Kitten / Pioneer Kitten / Lemon Sandstorm to pivot from compromised internet-facing appliances into internal networks.
author: detection-engineering
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.t1090
  - attack.g0117
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_bin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ngrok.exe'
      - '\frpc.exe'
      - '\frps.exe'
      - '\plink.exe'
      - '\chisel.exe'
      - '\ssf.exe'
      - '\revsocks.exe'
      - '\gost.exe'
  selection_interp:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'ngrok'
      - 'frpc'
      - 'plink'
      - 'chisel'
      - 'revsocks'
  selection_args:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'authtoken'
      - ' -R '
      - '--remote'
      - 'tunnel'
      - '-c frpc'
      - '--config'
      - 'tcp 3389'
      - 'tcp 445'
      - 'tcp 22'
      - ' -L '
      - '--reverse'
      - '--server'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_bin or selection_interp) and selection_args and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned ngrok / FRP usage by developers (allowlist by host)
  - Pen-testers running approved engagements
level: high
ucs.rationale: Fox Kitten / Pioneer Kitten / Lemon Sandstorm (G0117) is one of the few advanced groups whose post-exploitation playbook is dominated by *open-source tunneling utilities* rather than custom implants — CISA AA20-259A, ClearSky, and CrowdStrike all enumerate FRP, ngrok, plink and similar SOCKS/SSH-tunnel binaries by name. The combination of (a) one of these specific binaries, (b) a reverse-tunnel ar
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Fox Kitten domain database (NTDS.dit) extraction via ntdsutil IFM / vssadmin shadow copy on a Domain Controller
ucs.description: Hunts the credential-access stage Fox Kitten consistently performs once they reach a DC: dumping the Active Directory database via 'ntdsutil ifm', creating a VSS snapshot to copy ntds.dit + SYSTEM hive offline, or using esentutl/wbadmin to lift the same files. ClearSky's Fox Kitten and Pay2Kitten reports document this NTDS.dit + SYSTEM hive exfil pattern as a recurring step before lateral movement and (in later campaigns) ransomware partner handoff.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND ( (Processes.process_name="ntdsutil.exe" AND Processes.process="*ifm*" AND Processes.process="*create*") OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*" AND Processes.process="*shadow*") OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*ntds*") OR (Processes.process_name="esentutl.exe" AND (Processes.process="*ntds.dit*" OR Processes.process="*/y*" OR Processes.process="*/vss*")) OR (Processes.process_name="reg.exe" AND Processes.process="*save*" AND (Processes.process="*HKLM\\SYSTEM*" OR Processes.process="*HKLM\\SAM*" OR Processes.process="*HKLM\\SECURITY*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*Win32_ShadowCopy*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-NinjaCopy*" OR Processes.process="*Copy-VSS*")) ) by host Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let DCFlag = DeviceInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | summarize arg_max(Timestamp, *) by DeviceId
    | where DeviceName has_any ("dc", "dom") or AdditionalFields has "DomainController"
    | project DCDeviceId = DeviceId;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ifm" and ProcessCommandLine has "create")
     or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadow")
     or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ntds")
     or (FileName =~ "esentutl.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ntds.dit"," /y"," /vss"))
     or (FileName =~ "reg.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "save"
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("HKLM\\SYSTEM","HKLM\\SAM","HKLM\\SECURITY","hklm\\system","hklm\\sam","hklm\\security"))
     or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Win32_ShadowCopy","Invoke-NinjaCopy","Copy-VSS","DSInternals"))
| join kind=leftouter DCFlag on $left.DeviceId == $right.DCDeviceId
| extend OnDC = isnotempty(DCDeviceId)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, OnDC, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel, SHA256
| order by OnDC desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let DCs = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4769          // Kerberos service ticket — DCs receive these constantly
    | summarize TgsCount = count() by Computer
    | where TgsCount > 1000
    | project Computer;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ProcName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where (ProcName == "ntdsutil.exe" and CommandLine has "ifm" and CommandLine has "create")
     or (ProcName == "vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "create" and CommandLine has "shadow")
     or (ProcName == "wbadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "ntds")
     or (ProcName == "esentutl.exe" and (CommandLine has "ntds.dit" or CommandLine has " /y" or CommandLine has " /vss"))
     or (ProcName == "reg.exe" and CommandLine has "save"
         and (CommandLine has "HKLM\\SYSTEM" or CommandLine has "HKLM\\SAM" or CommandLine has "HKLM\\SECURITY"))
     or (ProcName in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
         and (CommandLine has "Win32_ShadowCopy" or CommandLine has "Invoke-NinjaCopy"
              or CommandLine has "Copy-VSS" or CommandLine has "DSInternals"))
| extend OnDC = Computer in (DCs)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, OnDC, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by OnDC desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Domain database extraction via ntdsutil IFM and shadow-copy harvesting of ntds.dit + SYSTEM hive is a near-mandatory step in Fox Kitten's intrusion lifecycle, called out by name in CISA AA20-259A, ClearSky's Fox Kitten / Pay2Kitten reports, and CrowdStrike's PIONEER KITTEN profile — they consistently dump the AD database before pivoting to lateral movement (T1021.001/.002) and, in later campaigns,
